baseline_test_note: Baseline not run during finalize. Executor should record current pass/fail state from `pytest tests/gpu_orchestrator -q` before making edits, then re-run after edits to confirm the same set passes plus the newly added sentinel-skip test.
meta_commentary: Targeted single-file edit in gpu_orchestrator/runpod/worker_startup.template.sh (lines 342-345) plus one new pytest assertion. Five non-negotiable invariants from the brief and plan: (1) the sentinel hash MUST cover all five inputs — uv.lock contents, pyproject.toml contents, python: 3.10, extras: cuda124, and `"$UV_BIN" --version` output. Missing any input means silent drift. (2) Sentinel path is literally `.venv/.sync-inputs` so `rm -rf .venv` self-heals. (3) `rm -f "$SYNC_SENTINEL"` runs BEFORE the sync, and the sentinel write runs AFTER the sync — `set -e` at script top guarantees the post-sync write is unreachable on a non-zero `uv sync` exit. (4) `update_worker_phase "deps_verified"` (current line ~347) must remain OUTSIDE the new if/else so it fires on both skip and sync paths. (5) `--locked` must remain on the actual `uv sync` invocation. The submodule reconcile block at lines 270–293 must NOT be touched (commit 134e3ec already shipped that). The prior-mistake landmine: comparison must be on file-content semantics (sha256 string equality), never on a proxy exit code — the recent `git submodule status >/dev/null` bug (134e3ec) returned exit 0 on the broken state. Use the exact bash block from Step 2 of the plan; do not improvise. After editing, run `bash -n` on the rendered script first (cheapest catch for syntax typos), then the focused test, then the broader gpu_orchestrator suite. Do NOT add `pyproject.toml` to the hash as a comment-only mention — it must actually be `sha256sum pyproject.toml` in the rendered script (FLAG-001 fix). The new test must assert both `sha256sum uv.lock` and `sha256sum pyproject.toml` substrings appear. Final task includes a short throwaway script: render the script in two configs (matching/non-matching sentinel) and confirm the if-branch logic — then delete the script.
watch_items: Sentinel hash MUST include all 5 inputs: sha256sum uv.lock, sha256sum pyproject.toml, python: 3.10, extras: cuda124, and `"$UV_BIN" --version` — missing any silently bypasses drift detection.
FLAG-001 resolution: pyproject.toml must be hashed (not just commented). Without it, a pyproject edit that doesn't touch uv.lock would skip the sync and bypass `--locked`'s drift check.
Sentinel path must be literally `.venv/.sync-inputs` (under .venv/ so `rm -rf .venv` self-heals).

`rm -f "$SYNC_SENTINEL"` must run BEFORE `"$UV_BIN" sync --locked …`; the sentinel write must run AFTER it. set -e at script top makes the post-sync write unreachable on failure — no explicit exit-code check needed.
`update_worker_phase "deps_verified"` (current line ~347) must remain OUTSIDE the new if/else so it fires on both skip and sync paths.
`--locked` must remain on the actual `uv sync` invocation (lockfile-drift backstop preserved).
DO NOT touch the submodule reconcile block at lines 270–293 (already shipped in commit 134e3ec).
Comparison MUST be on file content (sha256 string equality), NEVER on a proxy exit code — see commit 134e3ec where `git submodule status >/dev/null` returned 0 on the broken state.
First-time provisioning (no .venv, no sentinel): the `[ -d .venv ] && [ -f "$SYNC_SENTINEL" ]` guard must naturally fall through to full sync.
Skip-path log line must include the computed hash and uv version so log readers can diagnose unexpected skip/non-skip.

Existing test `test_rendered_startup_script_bootstraps_uv_and_runs_locked_sync` must still pass — the literal `"$UV_BIN" sync --locked --python 3.10 --extra cuda124` and `touch .uv-migrated` are preserved.
`tr -d '[:space:]'` strips trailing newline from recorded hash — without it the comparison would always mismatch.
`echo "<key>: MISSING"` for absent uv.lock/pyproject.toml — degrades to a deterministic value rather than producing the same hash silently.
Assumption: sha256sum is in coreutils on the RunPod Ubuntu image — if absent, fall back to `python3 -c 'import hashlib …'`.
Verifiability flag (verifiability-0): runtime log verification of the skip line on a real warm boot is human/info-level only; CI cannot verify it.

sense_checks.question: In the edited gpu_orchestrator/runpod/worker_startup.template.sh, does the new block hash all five inputs literally — `sha256sum uv.lock`, `sha256sum pyproject.toml`, `python: 3.10`, `extras: cuda124`, and `"$UV_BIN" --version` — and is the sentinel path literally `.venv/.sync-inputs`?

Is `rm -f "$SYNC_SENTINEL"` placed BEFORE the `"$UV_BIN" sync --locked --python 3.10 --extra cuda124` invocation in the else-branch, and is the sentinel write (`printf '%s\n' "$EXPECTED_INPUTS_HASH" > "$SYNC_SENTINEL"`) placed AFTER it? And does `update_worker_phase "deps_verified"` sit OUTSIDE the new if/else (after the closing fi)?

Are the submodule reconcile block at lines 270–293 and the `=== VALIDATING IMPORTS ===` block (line ~349) both untouched? Confirm via `git diff` that the only changes are within the lines 342–345 region plus the test file.

Does the new pytest assertion in tests/gpu_orchestrator/runpod/test_startup_script.py pin all five hash inputs (asserting `sha256sum uv.lock`, `sha256sum pyproject.toml`, `python: 3.10`, `extras: cuda124`, `"$UV_BIN" --version` substrings) AND assert the rm-before-sync, write-after-sync ordering AND assert `SYNC_SENTINEL=".venv/.sync-inputs"`?

Does the rendered script parse cleanly under `bash -n` (no syntax errors)? Does the focused `pytest tests/gpu_orchestrator/runpod/test_startup_script.py -q` pass? Does the broader `pytest tests/gpu_orchestrator -q` pass with no new failures vs. baseline? Was a throwaway repro script written that proves the if-branch chooses skip when the sentinel matches and sync when it doesn't, then deleted?

tasks.description: In gpu_orchestrator/runpod/worker_startup.template.sh, replace the 4-line dependency-sync block (currently lines 342–345: the `=== DEPENDENCY SYNC (uv) ===` echo, the `"$UV_BIN" sync --locked --python 3.10 --extra cuda124 …` call, the `touch .uv-migrated`, and the `✅ uv sync complete; sentinel refreshed` echo) with the sentinel-skip bash block specified verbatim in plan Step 2. The block must: (a) define `SYNC_SENTINEL=".venv/.sync-inputs"`; (b) compute `UV_VERSION_STR` from `"$UV_BIN" --version 2>/dev/null || echo unknown`; (c) compute `EXPECTED_INPUTS_HASH` as a sha256 over the five lines `uv-version: …`, `python: 3.10`, `extras: cuda124`, `uv-lock-sha256: <sha256sum uv.lock | awk '{print $1}'>` (or `MISSING`), `pyproject-sha256: <sha256sum pyproject.toml | awk '{print $1}'>` (or `MISSING`); (d) guard with `if [ -d .venv ] && [ -f "$SYNC_SENTINEL" ]`, read recorded hash via `cat "$SYNC_SENTINEL" 2>/dev/null | tr -d '[:space:]'`, set `SYNC_SKIPPED=1` only on exact string match; (e) on skip: log `⏭️  Skipping uv sync: $SYNC_SENTINEL matches current inputs (hash=$EXPECTED_INPUTS_HASH; uv=$UV_VERSION_STR)` to `$LOG_FILE`; (f) on sync: `rm -f "$SYNC_SENTINEL" 2>/dev/null || true` BEFORE running `"$UV_BIN" sync --locked --python 3.10 --extra cuda124 >> "$LOG_FILE" 2>&1`, then write `printf '%s\n' "$EXPECTED_INPUTS_HASH" > "$SYNC_SENTINEL"` AFTER, then log `✅ uv sync complete; $SYNC_SENTINEL refreshed (hash=$EXPECTED_INPUTS_HASH; uv=$UV_VERSION_STR)`; (g) `touch .uv-migrated` outside the if/else so it always runs. The `update_worker_phase "deps_verified"` line and `=== VALIDATING IMPORTS ===` block downstream stay untouched. The submodule reconcile block at lines 270–293 stays untouched.

Add a new test in tests/gpu_orchestrator/runpod/test_startup_script.py named `test_rendered_startup_script_gates_uv_sync_on_inputs_sentinel` (per plan Step 3) that renders the script via `startup_script.render_startup_script(...)` and asserts: (a) `'SYNC_SENTINEL=".venv/.sync-inputs"' in script`; (b) all five hash-input substrings present — `'"$UV_BIN" --version'`, `'python: 3.10'`, `'extras: cuda124'`, `'sha256sum uv.lock'`, `'sha256sum pyproject.toml'`; (c) ordering invariants — `script.index('rm -f "$SYNC_SENTINEL"') < script.index('"$UV_BIN" sync --locked') < script.index('printf \'%s\\n\' "$EXPECTED_INPUTS_HASH" > "$SYNC_SENTINEL"')`; (d) skip-branch log present and ordered before the sync invocation — `script.index('⏭️  Skipping uv sync') < script.index('"$UV_BIN" sync --locked')`. The pre-existing test `test_rendered_startup_script_bootstraps_uv_and_runs_locked_sync` must remain unmodified and continue to pass (its assertions on the literal `"$UV_BIN" sync --locked --python 3.10 --extra cuda124`, `touch .uv-migrated`, and `update_worker_phase "deps_verified"` substrings are still satisfied by the new block).

Validate the change. Run in this order: (1) shell-parse the rendered script: `python -c "from gpu_orchestrator.runpod import startup_script; import sys; sys.stdout.write(startup_script.render_startup_script(worker_id='w', supabase_url='u', supabase_anon_key='a', supabase_service_key='s', replicate_api_token='r', max_task_wait_minutes=1, has_pending_tasks=False))" | bash -n` — must exit 0. (2) `pytest tests/gpu_orchestrator/runpod/test_startup_script.py -q` — must pass including the new test. (3) `pytest tests/gpu_orchestrator -q` — must pass with no new failures vs. baseline (record baseline state before T1 if not already known; previously-failing tests unrelated to this change are acceptable). (4) Throwaway repro: write a short Python script in /tmp that renders the startup script, then for two synthetic states (`mkdir -p .venv && echo MATCHING > .venv/.sync-inputs` vs. no sentinel), runs the new block under `bash` (with stubbed `uv`/sha256sum or by isolating just the if-branch logic) to confirm the skip path is taken iff the sentinel matches the freshly computed hash; print PASS/FAIL; then delete the script. If any test or check fails, read the error, fix the code in T1/T2, and re-run until green. Do NOT create new persistent test files — only edit the existing test_startup_script.py.

plan_steps_covered.plan_step_summary: Step 1: Confirm exact insertion point and surrounding invariants (read-only sanity check before edit)
Step 2: Replace the dependency-sync block (lines 342–345) with the sentinel-skip bash logic per the plan's verbatim block
Step 3: Add a new pytest assertion pinning the sentinel-skip contract (path, 5 hash inputs, rm-before-sync/write-after-sync ordering, skip-log presence)
Step 4: Validate via `bash -n` on rendered script, focused pytest, broader gpu_orchestrator pytest, plus a throwaway repro

validation.completeness_notes: Plan Step 1 (read-only landmark confirmation) is folded into T1 rather than given a separate task — per the finalize-time guidance to keep task count proportional, an executor naturally reads the surrounding lines before editing them, and creating a separate inspect-only task would be ceremony. Plan Step 4's runtime-log check (`⏭️  Skipping uv sync` on a real warm boot) is the info-level / verifiability-0 item — not a CI-verifiable task — so it lives in watch_items, not as a task. All 'must' success criteria from the plan metadata are covered by T1+T2+T3.
